evidence.title: Agentic Workflow Automation for Industrial IT: The 2026 Guide
evidence.snippet: The same pattern appears across agentic AI in mining operations: facilities waiting 12 to 18 months for reporting a developer could build in a week. Agentic workflow automation operates in a continuous loop. ## Agentic Workflow Use Cases in Industrial Operations. Enterprise agentic AI requires four governance layers: a staging environment where solutions are built in isolation from production, a risk assessment step to check for vulnerabilities before IT review, IT approval gates that control what reaches production, and full audit trails with rollback capability. With a governed agentic AI platform like Opsima Agent Builder, workflows that previously took 6 to 24 months through traditional IT channels can be deployed in days. Real customer examples: a major container terminal’s 12-month integration backlog was replaced with agent-built workflows deployed in a fraction of the time. Yes. Agentic workflow automation platforms designed for industrial IT build the integration layer on top of existing enterprise systems.

evidence.title: Spanish startup Supersonik launches from stealth and raises €4.2 ...
evidence.snippet: # Spanish startup Supersonik launches from stealth and raises €4.2 million for its multilingual AI agent | EU-Startups. HomeFundingSpanish startup Supersonik launches from stealth and raises €4.2 million for its... # Spanish startup Supersonik launches from stealth and raises €4.2 million for its multilingual AI agent. Launched today from stealth with €4.2 million in Seed funding, Barcelona’s **Supersonik** is introducing an autonomous, multilingual AI agent that joins live video calls, showcases real software in action, and adapts the demo to the buyer in real time. “_We’re living through the biggest tech shift of our lifetime with AI, and teaming up with Dani and Pol was a no-brainer to build a future where every company can engage buyers instantly, globally, and at scale, transforming how software is discovered and bought,_” added **Joaquim Lechà**, Co-founder at Supersonik. ### German HealthTech startup Recare closes €37 million funding round to scale AI platform and expand internationally.

evidence.title: Meet Maisa, The Spanish AI Start-Up Backed By The Titans Of Big ...
evidence.snippet: Our best stories, exclusive reporting and Forbes perspectives on the day’s top news. “Our view is that the approach of many AI companies just isn’t going to work because they are asking enterprises to blindly trust their models,” says David Villalón, co-founder and CEO of Maisa. He points to recent research from Amazon, which found that while 25% of businesses had begun experimenting with generative AI, just 6% had put the technology to use in a live production environment.
